info.name: FlowShift API
info.description:
## FlowShift — iPaaS Migration Playbook Generator

FlowShift converts workflows between iPaaS platforms (n8n, Make, Zapier, Tray, Boomi, Workato, Celigo) using Claude AI. Submit a source workflow — as a file or plain-text description — and receive a detailed migration playbook plus a ready-to-import destination workflow file.

---

### Authentication

All `/api/*` endpoints require an `X-API-Key` header. The key is returned **once** when you register and is stored in the `{{api_key}}` collection variable automatically.

```
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
```

---

### Quick start

1. **Register** — run *Register User* to create an account. `api_key` and `user_id` are saved to collection variables automatically.
2. **Submit a run** — run *Create Run (description only)* or *Create Run (with file content)*. The `run_id` is saved automatically.
3. **Poll for results** — run *Get Run* every few seconds until `status` is `completed`. The response includes the full playbook text and import file content.
4. **Browse history** — run *List User Runs* at any time to see all past migrations.

---

### Collection variables

| Variable | Set by | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | You | API base URL (default: `https://flowshift-cdl.fly.dev`) |
| `api_key` | Register User (auto) | Your API key — required on all authenticated requests |
| `user_id` | Register User (auto) | Your user ID |
| `run_id` | Create Run (auto) | Most recently created run ID |

---

### Run status lifecycle

```
pending → processing → completed
                    ↘ failed
```

`POST /api/runs` returns `202 Accepted` with `status: pending` immediately. Claude processes the migration in the background (typically 30–60 seconds). Poll `GET /api/runs/:id` until status reaches a terminal state.

variable.description: API base URL. Change to http://localhost:8080 for local development.
Your API key. Populated automatically by the Register User test script. Store it securely — it is shown only once.
Your user ID (UUID). Populated automatically by the Register User test script.
The most recently created run ID. Populated automatically by the Create Run test scripts. Used by Get Run.

item.name: Health
item.description: Liveness check. Use this to confirm the API server is reachable and the Node.js process has started successfully. No authentication required.

request.description:
Returns the service name and current server timestamp.

**Authentication:** None required.

**Use this to:**
- Verify the server is up before running other requests
- Check the server clock (useful for debugging timestamp fields in run responses)
- Confirm a fresh deployment is healthy

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | string | Always `"ok"` when the process is running |
| `service` | string | Always `"flowshift-api"` |
| `timestamp` | string | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the server at request time |

item.name: Users
item.description:
User registration and profile management.

Users are identified by a UUID and authenticated via an API key. The API key is returned **once** at registration time and cannot be retrieved again — store it securely. If lost, you will need to register a new account.

All endpoints in this folder except *Register User* require the `X-API-Key` header.

exec: pm.test('api_key and user_id saved to collection variables', () => {
console.log('API key saved. Store it securely — it will not be shown again.');

request.description:
Create a new user account. Returns an `api_key` that must be included in the `X-API-Key` header on all subsequent requests.

**Authentication:** None required.

**Important:** The `api_key` is returned **only once** in this response. The built-in test script automatically saves it to the `{{api_key}}` collection variable. Store it separately in a secure location — it cannot be retrieved again.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `email` | string | ✓ | Must be a valid email address. Must be unique — registering with an existing email returns 409. |
| `name` | string | | Display name shown in the dashboard. |

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string (UUID) | Your user ID — used in all `/api/users/:id/*` endpoints |
| `email` | string | Normalized to lowercase |
| `name` | string \| null | Display name |
| `api_key` | string (UUID) | Your API key — **shown once only** |

response.name: 400 Bad Request — missing or invalid email
409 Conflict — email already registered

request.description:
Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user based on the `X-API-Key` header. This endpoint is used by the sign-in flow to look up a user when only the API key is known (i.e., the user ID is not yet in localStorage).

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string (UUID) | User ID |
| `email` | string | Email address |
| `name` | string \| null | Display name |
| `gdrive_folder_id` | string \| null | Google Drive folder ID. Null until the first run completes with Drive enabled. |
| `gdrive_folder_url` | string \| null | Shareable Drive folder URL. Set alongside `gdrive_folder_id`. |
| `created_at` | string | ISO 8601 UTC creation timestamp |

**Note:** The `api_key` field is intentionally excluded from this response.

Fetch a user's full profile by their UUID. The authenticated user may only fetch their own profile — attempting to fetch another user's profile returns 403.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:id` | User UUID (stored in `{{user_id}}`) |

**Response fields:** Same as *Get Current User (me)*.

**Errors:**
- `401` — missing or invalid API key
- `403` — the API key belongs to a different user than the `:id` in the path

Returns a summary list of all migration runs for the authenticated user, ordered newest first. This endpoint returns lightweight summary objects — it does not include `playbook_text` or `import_file_content`. Use `GET /api/runs/:id` to fetch full results for a specific run.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:id` | User UUID (stored in `{{user_id}}`) |

**Run summary fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string (UUID) | Run ID — use with `GET /api/runs/:id` |
| `source` | string | Source platform |
| `destination` | string | Destination platform |
| `status` | string | `pending` \| `processing` \| `completed` \| `failed` |
| `original_filename` | string \| null | Name of the uploaded file, if any |
| `gdrive_run_folder_url` | string \| null | Drive folder URL. Null if Drive is not authorized or run hasn't completed. |
| `error_message` | string \| null | Error details when `status` is `failed` |
| `created_at` | string | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp |
| `completed_at` | string \| null | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. Null while pending or processing. |

item.name: Runs
item.description:
Migration run creation and result retrieval.

A run represents a single iPaaS migration job. The flow is:
1. `POST /api/runs` — create a run (returns `202` immediately, processing is async)
2. `GET /api/runs/:id` — poll every 3–5 seconds until `status` is `completed` or `failed`

**Supported platforms:** `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo`

When a run completes, two files are available in the response and in Google Drive:
- **`playbook.md`** — step-by-step migration guide in markdown
- **`flowshift_<src>_to_<dst>.json`** — ready-to-import workflow file for the destination platform (where supported)

**Note on Zapier as destination:** Zapier does not support workflow import. For Zapier destinations, `import_file_content` will be null and `has_import_file` will be false.

exec: console.log('Run created:', body.id, '— now poll GET /api/runs/:id for results');

request.description:
Start a migration run by describing the source workflow in plain text. No file is required — Claude will use the description to generate the playbook and import file.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `source` | string | ✓ | Source platform. Must be one of: `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo` |
| `destination` | string | ✓ | Destination platform. Same valid values. Must differ from `source`. |
| `description` | string | one of † | Plain-text description of the workflow — triggers, actions, conditions, connected apps. |
| `fileContent` | string | one of † | Full text content of the source workflow file (see *Create Run with file content*). |

† At least one of `description` or `fileContent` must be provided. Both may be included together.

**Response:** `202 Accepted` with the run object at `status: pending`. The `run_id` is saved automatically by the test script. Processing takes 30–60 seconds — poll *Get Run* until `status` changes.

response.name: 202 Accepted — run queued
response.body:
{
  "id": "09944998-fd18-4f9f-a603-9d87efebddfb",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",
  "source": "Zapier",
  "destination": "n8n",
  "description": "When a new row is added to Google Sheets, send a Slack message to #general with the row data.",
  "original_filename": null,
  "status": "pending",
  "playbook_text": null,
  "import_file_name": null,
  "import_file_extension": null,
  "import_file_content": null,
  "has_import_file": false,
  "gdrive_run_folder_url": null,
  "error_message": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-28 17:06:21",
  "completed_at": null
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request — same source and destination
400 Bad Request — no description or file

request.description:
Start a migration run by providing the full text content of the source workflow file. The file is read client-side and sent as a JSON string — no binary upload is involved.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `source` | string | ✓ | Source platform |
| `destination` | string | ✓ | Destination platform |
| `fileContent` | string | one of † | The complete text content of the workflow file (JSON, YAML, XML, or TXT). Paste the raw file text here. |
| `fileName` | string | with `fileContent` | Original filename including extension (e.g. `my_workflow.json`). Used to name the uploaded file in Google Drive. |
| `description` | string | one of † | Optional plain-text description. Can be combined with `fileContent` for better context. |

† At least one of `fileContent` or `description` must be provided.

**Tip:** To test with a real workflow, open the file in a text editor, copy the entire contents, and paste into `fileContent` as a JSON string (remember to escape quotes and newlines, or use a JSON serialiser).

response.name: 202 Accepted — run queued
response.body:
{
  "id": "1e7054cd-5195-4db9-971a-7634a8af2e12",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",
  "source": "n8n",
  "destination": "Make",
  "description": null,
  "original_filename": "my_workflow.json",
  "status": "pending",
  "playbook_text": null,
  "import_file_name": null,
  "import_file_extension": null,
  "import_file_content": null,
  "has_import_file": false,
  "gdrive_run_folder_url": null,
  "error_message": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-28 17:10:00",
  "completed_at": null
}

request.description:
Fetch the full details and results of a specific run. Poll this endpoint every 3–5 seconds after creating a run until `status` reaches `completed` or `failed`.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required. Users can only fetch their own runs.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:id` | Run UUID (stored in `{{run_id}}` after Create Run) |

**Status lifecycle:**
```
pending → processing → completed
                    ↘ failed
```

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | Run UUID |
| `user_id` | string | Owning user UUID |
| `source` | string | Source platform |
| `destination` | string | Destination platform |
| `description` | string \| null | Plain-text description, if provided |
| `original_filename` | string \| null | Uploaded filename, if provided |
| `status` | string | `pending` \| `processing` \| `completed` \| `failed` |
| `playbook_text` | string \| null | Full migration playbook in markdown. Populated when `completed`. |
| `import_file_content` | string \| null | Ready-to-import workflow file text. Null if destination doesn't support import (e.g. Zapier). |
| `import_file_name` | string \| null | Suggested filename for the import file (e.g. `flowshift_zapier_to_n8n.json`) |
| `import_file_extension` | string \| null | File extension without dot (e.g. `json`) |
| `has_import_file` | boolean | Convenience flag — true when `import_file_content` is non-null |
| `gdrive_run_folder_url` | string \| null | Google Drive folder URL containing `playbook.md` and the import file. Null if Drive is not authorized. |
| `error_message` | string \| null | Error details when `status` is `failed` |
| `created_at` | string | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp |
| `completed_at` | string \| null | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. Null while pending or processing. |

response.name: 200 OK — completed
response.body:
{
  "id": "09944998-fd18-4f9f-a603-9d87efebddfb",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",
  "source": "Zapier",
  "destination": "n8n",
  "description": "When a new row is added to Google Sheets, send a Slack message to #general.",
  "original_filename": null,
  "status": "completed",
  "playbook_text": "# Flowshift Migration Playbook\n\n## Migration Overview\n...",
  "import_file_name": "flowshift_zapier_to_n8n.json",
  "import_file_extension": "json",
  "import_file_content": "{\"name\": \"Google Sheets → Slack #general\", \"nodes\": [...]}",
  "has_import_file": true,
  "gdrive_run_folder_url": "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ILBWqtebGL9GI3kZTp21CBktxmBDqWVI",
  "error_message": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-28 17:06:21",
  "completed_at": "2026-05-28 17:06:53"
}

item.name: Auth (Google Drive)
item.description:
Google OAuth2 flow for authorizing Drive uploads.

FlowShift uses OAuth2 (not a service account) to write files to your personal Google Drive. This is a one-time browser-based authorization. After you approve, the refresh token is stored in the database and reused automatically for all future runs.

These endpoints are browser-redirect flows — they are not designed to be called from Postman directly (the `GET /auth/google` response is a redirect to Google). They are included here for documentation and reference.

request.description:
Initiates the Google OAuth2 authorization flow. This endpoint redirects the browser to Google's consent screen.

**Authentication:** None required (this IS the authentication flow).

**How to use:**
1. Open `{{base_url}}/auth/google` in a browser (do not use Postman's built-in browser for this)
2. Sign in with the Google account that owns the Drive folder
3. Grant Drive access on the consent screen
4. You will be redirected to `/auth/google/callback`, which stores the refresh token and shows a confirmation page

**Prerequisites:**
- `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` must be set as Fly.io secrets
- The GCP OAuth client must have `{{base_url}}/auth/google/callback` as an authorized redirect URI
- Your Google account email must be in the GCP project's test users list (until the app is Google-verified)

**This is a one-time setup.** The refresh token persists across server restarts and redeployments. Re-authorize only if the token is revoked (e.g. you remove Drive access from your Google account).
